{
 "cells": [
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "from sklearn.model_selection import StratifiedKFold\n",
    "\n",
    "input_file_path = \"/workspace/code/data/astmh2023AbstractContents_22mar2024.xlsx\""
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Read Excel file\n",
    "df = pd.read_excel(input_file_path)\n",
    "# Define features and labels\n",
    "X = df[['abstractId','generalCategory','title', 'abstractText', 'mergedCategory', 'category']]\n",
    "y = df[['mergedCategory']]\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 9,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Initialize 5-fold cross-validation\n",
    "skf = StratifiedKFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Fold 1:\n",
      "Train Set saved to train_split_merged_1.xlsx - X: 1822, y: 1822\n",
      "Test Set saved to test_split_merged_1.xlsx - X: 456, y: 456\n",
      "==============================\n",
      "Fold 2:\n",
      "Train Set saved to train_split_merged_2.xlsx - X: 1822, y: 1822\n",
      "Test Set saved to test_split_merged_2.xlsx - X: 456, y: 456\n",
      "==============================\n",
      "Fold 3:\n",
      "Train Set saved to train_split_merged_3.xlsx - X: 1822, y: 1822\n",
      "Test Set saved to test_split_merged_3.xlsx - X: 456, y: 456\n",
      "==============================\n",
      "Fold 4:\n",
      "Train Set saved to train_split_merged_4.xlsx - X: 1823, y: 1823\n",
      "Test Set saved to test_split_merged_4.xlsx - X: 455, y: 455\n",
      "==============================\n",
      "Fold 5:\n",
      "Train Set saved to train_split_merged_5.xlsx - X: 1823, y: 1823\n",
      "Test Set saved to test_split_merged_5.xlsx - X: 455, y: 455\n",
      "==============================\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Generate cross-validation splits\n",
    "for fold, (train_index, test_index) in enumerate(skf.split(X, y), 1):\n",
    "    X_train, X_test = X.iloc[train_index], X.iloc[test_index]\n",
    "    y_train, y_test = y.iloc[train_index], y.iloc[test_index]\n",
    "    \n",
    "    train_by_split = pd.concat([X_train, y_train], axis=1)\n",
    "    test_by_split = pd.concat([X_test, y_test], axis=1)\n",
    "\n",
    "    # Save train and test splits as new Excel files\n",
    "    train_filename = f'train_split_merged_{fold}.xlsx'\n",
    "    test_filename = f'test_split_merged_{fold}.xlsx'\n",
    "    train_by_split.to_excel(\"/workspace/code/data/\" + train_filename, index=True, header=True)\n",
    "    test_by_split.to_excel(\"/workspace/code/data/\" + test_filename, index=True, header=True)\n",
    "    \n",
    "    print(f\"Fold {fold}:\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Train Set saved to {train_filename} - X: {len(X_train)}, y: {len(y_train)}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"Test Set saved to {test_filename} - X: {len(X_test)}, y: {len(y_test)}\")\n",
    "    print(\"=\"*30)\n",
    "    "
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 19,
   "metadata": {},
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Total Bins =  51\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure size 720x432 with 1 Axes>"
      ]
     },

      "needs_background": "light"
     },
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Visualize the histogram of categories and general categories \n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "\n",
    "# Read Excel file\n",
    "df = pd.read_excel(\"/workspace/code/data/test_split_merged_4.xlsx\")  # test or astmh2023AbstractContents_19mar2024\n",
    "\n",
    "# Specify the column for which you want to plot the histogram\n",
    "column_name = 'mergedCategory' #category  generalCategory\n",
    "print(\"Total Bins = \", len(df[column_name].unique()))\n",
    "\n",
    "# Plot histogram\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))\n",
    "plt.hist(df[column_name], bins=len(df[column_name].unique()), color='skyblue', edgecolor='black', stacked=True)\n",
    "plt.title(f'Histogram of {column_name}')\n",
    "plt.xlabel(column_name)\n",
    "plt.ylabel('Frequency')\n",
    "plt.grid(True)\n",
    "plt.show()"
   ]
  },

    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Relapses from latent liver-stage parasites (hypnozoites) are a key challenge for Plasmodium vivax elimination. Relapses are responsible for >80% of blood-stage infections. There are currently no tools to detect hypnozoites, but P. vivax blood-stage infections induce strong antibody responses arising quickly after infection and decaying slowly over time. Antibodies are thus markers of both current and recent past infections, allowing identification of people recently exposed to P. vivax and thus at risk of carrying hypnozoites. This study aimed to test the ability of antibody signatures to a validated set of 13 antigens to predict relapse risk in two cohorts of soldiers (n = 592) who returned to a malaria-free area from a 9-month deployment to a malaria-endemic area between 2018 and 2022. All soldiers were assessed by Luminex serology on the day of recruitment and followed actively every two weeks and at time of febrile symptoms until first recurrent P. vivax parasitaemia, for up to 6 months. An optimised machine learning Random Forest classification algorithm was used to classify soldiers as exposed during the previous 9 months. Over 110 soldiers experienced relapses during follow-up with significant heterogeneity in relapse risk between the two cohorts (Cohort 1: 25, Cohort 2: >85). Soldiers who relapsed had higher median antibody titers for most of the biomarkers compared to those who did not relapse. In cohort 1, our diagnostic tool had 75% sensitivity and 93% specificity at identifying future relapses using blood samples from recruitment day. Recurrent infections strongly boosted antibody titres both in sero-positive and initially sero-negative soldiers. Analyses of the 2nd cohort are ongoing but preliminary results indicate a comparable performance. This demonstrates the ability of serological markers to identify people at risk of relapse with high accuracy. In our presentation, we will present the final results across both cohorts and discuss the implications of these results for the development of a novel public health intervention P. vivax serological testing and treatment (PvSeroTAT) for relapse prevention.\n",
      "(768,)\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import random\n",
    "from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer\n",
    "model = SentenceTransformer('multi-qa-mpnet-base-dot-v1') # \n",
    "random_idx = random.randint(0, len(df['abstractText']))\n",
    "random_abs = df['abstractText'][random_idx]\n",
    "paragraph = [random_abs]\n",
    "\n",
    "embedding = model.encode(paragraph)\n",
    "print(random_abs)\n",
    "print(embedding[0].shape)"
   ]
  }
 ],
